notes: Confirmed: SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor only supports client-side WebAssembly — server-side Blazor rendering is not implemented. The package has [SupportedOSPlatform("browser")] on all view types, depends on SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly, and the JSModuleInterop throws NotSupportedException when IJSRuntime is not IJSInProcessRuntime (i.e., server-side Blazor). No server-side rendering code, SignalR marshaling, or alternative rendering pipeline exists in any version tested (2.88.9, 3.119.2, or main branch). Client-side WASM support was delivered via PR #1811, but the server-side portion of this feature request remains unaddressed.

reproductionSteps.description: Create Blazor Server app with SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor 2.88.9
reproductionSteps.command: dotnet new blazor -n Repro --framework net10.0 --interactivity server --no-https && cd Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor --version 2.88.9
reproductionSteps.output:
The template 'Blazor Web App' was created successfully.
PackageReference for package 'SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor' version '2.88.9' added.

filesCreated.description: Blazor Server page using SKCanvasView with InteractiveServer render mode

reproductionSteps.description: Build Blazor Server app — builds successfully but SKCanvasView is browser-only
Investigate SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor 2.88.9 nuspec — confirm WASM-only dependency
reproductionSteps.output: Package depends on SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly 2.88.9. Only targets net6.0. No server-side rendering code present.

reproductionSteps.description: Investigate JSModuleInterop source — confirm NotSupportedException for non-WASM runtimes
reproductionSteps.output: Pre-.NET 7: throws NotSupportedException('SkiaSharp currently only works on Web Assembly.') when IJSRuntime is not IJSInProcessRuntime. .NET 7+: uses JSHost.ImportAsync which is WASM-only. [SupportedOSPlatform("browser")] on class.

reproductionSteps.description: Investigate SKCanvasView source — confirm browser-only platform attribute
reproductionSteps.output: [SupportedOSPlatform("browser")] on SKCanvasView class. Uses GCHandle for pixel buffer transfer, ElementReference for HTML canvas, JS interop for putImageData — all require in-process browser execution. No fallback for server-side rendering.
<SupportedPlatform Include="browser" /> in csproj. ProjectReference to SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly. No server-side rendering project or code found.

reproductionSteps.description: Search for any server-side Blazor support code in main branch source
reproductionSteps.output: No server-related files found. Zero references to server-side Blazor, SignalR, or alternative rendering pipelines in SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor.

versionResults.notes: Package builds in Blazor Server project but is WASM-only. Depends on SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly. JSModuleInterop throws NotSupportedException for non-WASM runtimes.
Same WASM-only architecture. Package still depends on SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly for both net6.0 and net8.0 TFMs. [SupportedOSPlatform("browser")] still present. No server-side support added.
Main branch source has identical WASM-only constraints. No server-side Blazor rendering code, SignalR marshaling, or alternative rendering pipeline exists.

actionability.reason: Confirmed feature request: server-side Blazor support is genuinely missing from SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor. Client-side WASM was delivered (PR #1811), but the server-side portion of the original request remains unaddressed across all versions. The maintainer closed #1921 as a duplicate of this issue in Nov 2024, confirming this is the canonical tracking issue.

actions.description: Apply feature-request and Blazor area labels

workarounds: Use standard SkiaSharp on the server to render images, encode as PNG/JPEG, and send to the client as tags or via Blazor streaming
Use the maintainer's prototype repo (mattleibow/SkiaSharpBlazorComponents) as a starting point for server-side image rendering
For interactive scenarios, consider using Blazor Auto render mode (.NET 8+) which starts server-side then transitions to client WASM
